# terrible_netapp_ontap_na_ontap_vserver_cifs_security (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `vserver` (String) name of the vserver.

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `encryption_required_for_dc_connections` (Boolean) Specifies whether encryption is required for domain controller connections.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `is_aes_encryption_enabled` (Boolean) Determine whether AES-128 and AES-256 encryption mechanisms are enabled for Kerberos-related CIFS communication.
- `is_password_complexity_required` (Boolean) Determine whether password complexity is required for local users.
- `is_signing_required` (Boolean) Determine whether signing is required for incoming CIFS traffic.
- `is_smb_encryption_required` (Boolean) Determine whether SMB encryption is required for incoming CIFS traffic.
- `kerberos_clock_skew` (Number) The clock skew in minutes is the tolerance for accepting tickets with time stamps that do not exactly match the host's system clock.
- `kerberos_kdc_timeout` (Number) Determine the timeout value in seconds for KDC connections.
- `kerberos_renew_age` (Number) Determine the maximum amount of time in days for which a ticket can be renewed.
- `kerberos_ticket_age` (Number) Determine the maximum amount of time in hours that a user's ticket may be used for the purpose of Kerberos authentication.
- `lm_compatibility_level` (String) Determine the LM compatibility level.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `referral_enabled_for_ad_ldap` (Boolean) Determine whether LDAP referral chasing is enabled or not for AD LDAP connections.
- `session_security_for_ad_ldap` (String) Determine the level of security required for LDAP communications.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `smb1_enabled_for_dc_connections` (String) Determine if SMB version 1 is used for connections to domain controllers.
- `smb2_enabled_for_dc_connections` (String) Determine if SMB version 2 is used for connections to domain controllers.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `use_ldaps_for_ad_ldap` (Boolean) Determine whether to use LDAPS for secure Active Directory LDAP connections.
- `use_start_tls_for_ad_ldap` (Boolean) Determine whether to use start_tls for AD LDAP connections.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
